core.label: Erlang Fundamentals
core.description: Syntax, data types, pattern matching, and the BEAM VM.

topics.label: Installation & Shell
topics.description: Install Erlang/OTP and use the erl shell. Erlang runs on the BEAM virtual machine.

topics.label: Data Types
topics.description: Atoms, numbers, tuples, lists, maps, binaries, and PIDs. Atoms are named constants.

topics.label: Pattern Matching
topics.description: The = operator matches patterns. Pattern matching is used everywhere in Erlang.

topics.label: Functions & Modules
topics.description: Define functions in modules with -module and -export. Functions are identified by name/arity.

topics.label: Guards & Control Flow
topics.description: case, if, guards (when), and function clause matching. No loops — use recursion.

topics.label: Recursion & Tail Calls
topics.description: Iterate with recursive functions. Tail-call optimization makes recursion efficient.

topics.label: List Operations
topics.description: Head/tail ([H|T]), lists module functions, and list comprehensions.

topics.label: Higher-Order Functions
topics.description: fun/lambda syntax, lists:map, lists:filter, lists:foldl. Functions as values.

core.label: Concurrency & OTP
core.description: Processes, message passing, and OTP behaviours.

topics.label: Processes & spawn
topics.description: Create lightweight processes with spawn. Erlang processes are isolated with no shared memory.

topics.label: Message Passing
topics.description: Send (!) and receive messages between processes. The only way processes communicate.

topics.label: Links & Monitors
topics.description: Link processes for crash propagation, monitor for notifications. Foundation of fault tolerance.

topics.label: gen_server
topics.description: Generic server behaviour — handle_call, handle_cast, handle_info. The most used OTP behaviour.

topics.label: Supervisors
topics.description: Monitor and restart child processes. Supervision trees are Erlang's fault-tolerance model.

topics.label: Applications
topics.description: Package code as OTP applications with .app files.

topics.label: ETS
topics.description: In-memory key-value storage shared between processes. Extremely fast lookups.

topics.label: Error Handling & Let It Crash
topics.description: try/catch, throw, exit, and the philosophy of letting processes crash and restart.
